quotes.text: The aim of totalitarian education has never been to instill convictions but to destroy the capacity to form any.
quotes.author: — Hannah Arendt, The Origins of Totalitarianism

quotes.text: Who controls the past controls the future: who controls the present controls the past.
quotes.author: — George Orwell, 1984

quotes.text: The function of the intellect is to provide a map of the possible — and to trouble the waters of the given.
quotes.author: — James Baldwin, The Creative Process

quotes.text: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
quotes.author: — Albert Camus, Return to Tipasa

quotes.text: Ideas are dangerous, but the man who dies without an idea is the one who truly perishes.
quotes.author: — John Maynard Keynes, Essays in Persuasion

quotes.text: The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
quotes.author: — Ludwig Wittgenstein, Tractatus
